// Status: Core Kernel
// Purpose: The HyperRing Reactor (Event Loop).

use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::task::Waker;
use std::time::Duration;
use crate::error::Result;

/// Token used to correlate IO events with tasks.
type Token = usize;

/// The Core Reactor.
///
/// It replaces the traditional poll-based loop (epoll) with a submission-based
/// model (io_uring). It holds the registry of tasks waiting for I/O.
pub struct Reactor {
    /// Map of Token -> Waker.
    /// When an I/O event completes (e.g., ring completion queue event),
    /// we look up the Token and wake the associated task.
    registry: Mutex<HashMap<Token, Waker>>,
    
    /// Reference to the io_uring driver.
    /// Kept internal to prevent unsafe leakage.
    // driver: IoUringDriver, // Commented out to avoid circular deps in this file structure
}

impl Reactor {
    pub fn new() -> Arc<Self> {
        Arc::new(Self {
            registry: Mutex::new(HashMap::new()),
        })
    }

    /// Registers interest in an I/O event.
    ///
    /// This is called by the `Future::poll` method of leaf futures (like TcpStream).
    /// It stores the Waker so the Reactor can wake it later.
    pub fn register(&self, token: Token, waker: Waker) {
        let mut reg = self.registry.lock().unwrap();
        reg.insert(token, waker);
    }

    /// The main loop "tick".
    ///
    /// In a production environment, this blocks on `io_uring_wait_cqe`.
    /// For this reference, it simulates event processing.
    pub fn tick(&self) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Submit all pending SQEs to the kernel.
        // self.driver.submit()?;

        // 2. Wait for Completion Queue Entries (CQEs).
        // This call blocks until at least one event is ready or timeout.
        // let completions = self.driver.wait_for_completions()?;

        // 3. Process completions.
        // For demonstration, we assume a completion occurred for Token 0.
        let completed_tokens = vec![]; // mocked empty

        let mut reg = self.registry.lock().unwrap();
        for token in completed_tokens {
            if let Some(waker) = reg.remove(&token) {
                // LOCK-FREE WAKE:
                // We wake the task. The AtomicWaker implementation in task.rs
                // handles the safe transition to the run queue.
                waker.wake();
            }
        }

        Ok(())
    }
}

/// A global handle to the reactor is usually required for convenient 
/// Future implementation, though explicit passing is cleaner.
/// We use a thread-local or static in `lib.rs` usually.
pub struct ReactorHandle(pub Arc<Reactor>);
